channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Africa Forward Summit Brings 30+ African Leaders to Nairobi for Investment Talks with France
item.description: Nairobi hosted a major gathering as over 30 African heads of state and thousands of investors convened for the Africa Forward Business Forum. The two-day summit focused on exploring investment opportu...

item.title: South African Films Highlighted at 33rd New York African Film Festival
item.description: The 33rd edition of the New York African Film Festival (NYAFF) is once again showcasing the richness of African storytelling on an international stage, with South African films featuring prominently. ...

item.title: Labour faces internal tensions as Starmer seeks to maintain leadership
item.description: Sir Keir Starmer is confronting a tense period within the Labour Party as calls for his resignation emerge from a small number of MPs. Recent electoral setbacks have heightened anxiety among party mem...

item.title: Labour MPs urge Prime Minister to step down
item.description: The news article intended to report on the number of Labour MPs calling for the resignation of the Prime Minister and to identify those members. Unfortunately, the webpage is currently inaccessible, r...

item.title: One Nation secures first lower-house seat in Farrer byelection
item.description: In a landmark outcome for One Nation, the party secured a lower-house seat for the very first time in the Farrer byelection, signalling a major shift in voter sentiment. The victory came as locals exp...

item.title: Ministry clarifies Instagram glitch on Rahul Gandhi's post with Tamil Nadu CM Vijay
item.description: On 11th May 2026, the Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology (MeitY) clarified that it did not block Congress leader Rahul Gandhi's Instagram reel and photo featuring newly sworn-in Tamil ...

item.title: NCC supports Fadiel Adams, calls for due process amid arrest over Magaqa case
item.description: The National Coloured Congress (NCC) has publicly stood by its leader and Member of Parliament, Fadiel Adams, following his recent arrest in Cape Town. Adams faces charges including obstruction of jus...

item.title: Ongoing strike in Nova Scotia nursing homes raises concerns about resident care
item.description: Workers at several nursing homes in Nova Scotia continue to strike, prompting concern among families about the quality of care provided to residents. The strike stems from disputes over working condit...

item.title: Trump declines Iran’s proposal to end Gulf conflict; diplomatic talks continue
item.description: Washington/Islamabad: President Donald Trump has officially rejected Iran’s formal response to the latest US proposal aimed at ending the Gulf conflict, which was conveyed through Pakistani mediators....

item.title: PDP Plans Comeback in Kwara Under Saraki’s Leadership
item.description: After more than seven years wey PDP don lose grip for Kwara, dem dey try make comeback. The party don hold meeting for Abuja wey former Senate President Bukola Saraki organize, gather all the governor...

item.title: Win a Double Pass to Steven Spielberg’s Sci-Fi Thriller Disclosure Day
item.description: Australian subscribers of The Age are being offered a unique opportunity to claim one of 25 complimentary double passes to watch Steven Spielberg’s upcoming science-fiction film, Disclosure Day, in ci...

item.title: Nintendo stock drops amid Switch 2 price hike and cautious game lineup outlook
item.description: Nintendo's shares dropped 8 per cent in Tokyo following the announcement of a price increase for the upcoming Switch 2 console, coupled with concerns over a limited lineup of high-profile games. Despi...

item.title: Indian embassy confirms two Indian crew members from hantavirus‑affected MV Hondius are healthy and under quarantine in Netherlands
item.description: The Embassy of India in Spain has confirmed that two Indian crew members who were onboard the Dutch‑flagged cruise vessel MV Hondius, which reported a hantavirus outbreak, are ‘healthy and asymptomati...

item.title: Pakistan Emerges Stronger on Global Stage After Marka-e-Haq Victory
item.description: In an important statement from Washington, Pakistan’s envoy highlighted that the victory in Marka-e-Haq, the 19-day conflict with India in April-May 2025, has significantly improved Pakistan’s global ...

item.title: Modi urges citizens to adopt responsible habits to reduce economic impact of West Asia conflict
item.description: Prime Minister Narendra Modi has appealed to Indian citizens to revive certain work-from-home practices introduced during the Covid-19 pandemic as part of a broader call for 'nationally responsible' l...

item.title: Philippine authorities implement stricter border checks to prevent hantavirus
item.description: The Department of Health (DOH) announced that strict border screening measures are now in place at Philippine airports and seaports to prevent the entry of hantavirus into the country. This move follo...

item.title: Overton focuses on team success in IPL 2026 despite strong individual performance
item.description: Jamie Overton, the English all-rounder playing for Chennai Super Kings (CSK) in IPL 2026, has been showing remarkable improvement compared to his debut season with the team. In the match against Luckn...

item.title: Strategic Lessons from the Rideau Canal and the Strait of Hormuz
item.description: Father Raymond J. de Souza draws a historical parallel between the construction of the Rideau Canal in Canada and recent geopolitical events involving the Strait of Hormuz. He notes that Donald Trump'...

item.title: Evaluating Efficiency and Opportunities at the Philippine Orthopedic Center
item.description: The Philippine Orthopedic Center (POC) demonstrates a functioning healthcare system that, while not polished like private hospitals, effectively delivers care despite resource constraints. Observation...

item.title: PNP-CIDG issues subpoena for Senator Bato dela Rosa over alleged EJK cases
item.description: Senator Ronald “Bato” dela Rosa has been issued a subpoena by the Philippine National Police - Criminal Investigation and Detection Group (PNP-CIDG) in relation to alleged extrajudicial killings (EJKs...
